title: Real World Vehicles
wikitext:
A myriad of '''real world vehicles''' can be seen throughout [[Neon Genesis Evangelion]], [[End of Evangelion]], and [[Rebuild of Evangelion]]. These are usually military vehicles, sometimes civilian, but always drawn and animated with great detail to their features. Evangelion has a special relationship with warships, which go on their own page, although boats and small watercraft will remain on this page. See [[Warships in Evangelion]] for details on the warships of the series. == Ground vehicles == === Type-74 Nana-yon === The tank most commonly seen in the series, the Type-74http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Type_74_Nana-yon is the main battle tank of the JGSDF with 700 deployed as of 2006. It was originally designed during the 1960s, an era of tank design where it was assumed that HEAT (High-Explosive Anti-Tank) rounds had made most armor obsolete, and as such it has relatively thin armor that made the tank almost completely obsolete before the first production models even rolled off the assembly line in 1975. It is primarily armed with a Royal Ordnance L7 105 mm rifled gun, sports 120mm of armor, carries a crew of four, can travel at a speed of 53 km/h, and weighs 38 tonnes. In [[Resources:Explanation of Evangelion 1.01|Explanation of Evangelion 1.01]] it's called Type-76 Kai (modifed). It can be seen in [[Episode 1]], [[Episode 9]], [[End of Evangelion]], [[Evangelion 1.0]], and [[Evangelion 2.0]]. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 2 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:01_C005_big2.jpg|250px|]] |image2 = [[Image:01_C006_big.jpg|250px]] |caption = Type-74 tanks from [[FGC:Episode_01_Scene_01|Episode #01]]. }} [[Image:Type 74 tank.jpg|none|thumb|250px|A Type 74]] {{-}}

=== Type 90 MBT === This tank has been misidentified as a multitude of vehicles, among them a Leopard 2 A10 (fictional variant of the German [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leopard_2 Leopard 2], a British [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Challenger_2 Challenger 2], both based on the turret shape. However, turret armor is often and drastically changed across different versions (one such example being the Leopard 2 A4 compared to the A5), so the deciding factor is the layout of the hull, most notable features being the width to height ratio of the hull, the position of the driver's hatch, headlights and tow hooks. In real life, the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Type_90_Ky%C5%AB-maru Type 90]'s design is quite similar to that of the Leopard 2, having very similar armor layout to the A4 and using the same 120mm Rheinmetall L44 smoothbore gun also used by the American M1 Abrams, South Korean K1A1 and older versions of the Leopard 2. What sets it apart from other MBTs is its suspension system - a hydraulic one allowing for the hull to tilt forwards and backwards in order to reduce its profile when cresting hills - and the main gun having a conveyor belt style autoloader, reducing the crew to three men compared to the four most other MBTs have. The exact composition of the armor is a tightly kept secret, but it follows the principle of modular composite armor: Blocks of layered ceramic and steel plates intended to shatter impact rounds and dissipate the effect of hollow charges, that can be individually replaced when damaged, even as a field repair. Due to the overpressure system inside the crew compartment, the tank is NBC (nuclear, biological, chemical) proof, meaning it can withstand and protect the crew from gas attacks and even the aftereffects of a nuclear strike. It can be seen in [[Evangelion 1.0]] and [[Evangelion 2.0]]. [[Image:Type-90s.jpg|none|thumb|250px|Several Type 90s open fire in [[Evangelion 1.0]]. Note the reactive armor plates bolted onto the chassis and turret]]

=== T-80 === The T-80http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T-80 is a Soviet main battle tank developed from the T-64http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T-64, it entered service in 1976 and it was the first production tank in the world using a gas turbine engine for its propulsion, a full four years before the first appearance of the US M1 Abrams. The Russian army discontinued procurement and development of this tank because of its poor performance during the First Chechen War, but it's still under production for export, and diesel power variants with a much lower fuel consumption (one of the type's principal problems, shared with all gas turbine tanks) have been developed. The version shown in Evangelion 2.0 is called T-80UN in the [[Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection]], it's a fictional version. It can be seen in [[Evangelion 2.0]] during the battle against the [[Third Angel]]. [[Image:Eva2-22 C0012B tanks.jpg|left|thumb|220px|T-80s just before firing their main cannons in [[Evangelion 2.0]]]] [[Image:T-80 in Saint-Petersburg.jpg|none|thumb|220px|A T-80 in a Saint Petersburg museum]]

=== BM-30 Smerch === The BM-30 "Smerch"http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BM-30_Smerch (tornado) is a Russian-built multiple rocket launcher first designed in the 1980s that is currently used by 13 different countries, and whose Russian designation is 9A52. It is capable of firing 12 300mm missiles and rockets in 38 seconds over a maximum distance of 90km, with a total reload time of twenty minutes. It requires a crew of three, weighs 43.7 tons, and has a maximum speed of 60 km/h. It can be briefly seen in [[Evangelion 1.0]] during the initial attack on [[Sachiel]]. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 2 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:Smercheva1.jpg|250px|]] |image2 = [[Image:Smerch l1.jpg|250px|]] |caption = Smerch in [[Evangelion 1.0]] and in real life }} {{-}}

=== M-270 MLRS (Multiple Launch Rocket System) === The iconic M270http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M270_Multiple_Launch_Rocket_System, often referred to simply as the MLRS, is an American-made rocket artillery system first delivered to the US Army in 1983 that has since come to be used by 12 different countries, including 99 in use by the JGSDF. It can fire 12 rockets in less then 40 seconds or 2 missiles every ten seconds, and can be reloaded in either 4 minutes (M270) or 3 minutes (M270A1). It requires a crew of three, weighs 27.5 tons, and has a maximum speed of 50 mph (80.5 km/h). [[Image:M270A1-2.jpg|left|thumb|220px|Three MLRS firing at [[Sachiel]] in [[Evangelion 1.0]]]] [[Image:Army_mlrs_1982_02.jpg|none|thumb|100px|A MLRS firing]]

=== AMX-10 RC === A French tank destroyer and recon vehicle. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AMX_10_RC Several of these can be seen in EoE being used by the JSSDF to assault Nerv's defenses. [[Image:AMX 10 RC snapshot dvd EoE 00.11.00.png|left|thumb|220px|Three AMX-10 RC pointing their cannons in [[Episode_25'|Air]]]] [[Image:AMX-10-RC resized.jpg|none|thumb|220px|A French AMX-10 RC]]

=== Komatsu LAV === A Japanese light armored vehicle built by Komatsu Ltd. Defense Systems Division for the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Komatsu_LAV It appears in Evangelion 1.0. == Aircraft == === Sukhoi Su-33 Flanker-D === A Russian Navy fighter first introduced in 1994, the Su-33http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sukhoi_Su-33 can be seen prominently in [[Episode 08]]. This is rather different from the Su-33's real-life production run being limited to 36 aircraft (excluding prototypes). The animators have very faithfully depicted the nose landing gear of the Su-33 as seen on the right of the image from Episode 08, but this fidelity to detail ironically makes it clear that the STOBAR aircraft can't operate from an American Nimitz-class carrier in that configuration; they lack an American-pattern "tow bar" catapult attachment to their nose landing gear. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 2 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:08_C021_comp.jpg|300px|]] |image2 = [[Image:KnAAPO-Su-33-1.jpg|300px]] |caption = Su-33s from [[FGC:Episode_08_Scene_01|Episode #08]] compared with real-life Su-33s. Note the highly detailed landing gear on the Episode 08 image to the left, and the absence of a "tow bar" catapult attachment as seen on US carrier-borne aircraft. }} {{-}} === Sukhoi Su-34 Fullback === A twin-seat fighter bomber of the Russian Air Force. It first flew in 1990 and entered production in 2004 incorporating design aspects from other Sukhoi fighters including the Su-33.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sukhoi_Su-34 It appears in [[Evangelion 2.0]] in the role of escorting Unit-02 to Japan. And despite the fact that it is not designed for carrier flight operations it appears at the end of Evangelin 2.0 in the 3.0 next time preview on a Nimitz-class aircraft carrier. (It is worth noting that the animators misjudged the size of the aircraft - the aircraft seen on the carrier are approximately 70 percent the size of their real-world counterparts). {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 2 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:Eva2-22 C0136.jpg|300px|]] |image2 = [[Image:Crossbowdrop-Sukhoi-eva2.0.jpg|300px]] |image3 = [[Image:Eva3.0 NTP carrier.jpeg|300px]] |image4 = [[Image:Su-34.jpg|300px]] |caption = Su-34s from Evangelion 2.0 compared with real-life Su-34. }} {{-}} === Bell-Boeing V-22 Osprey === An American multi-purpose tilt rotor aircraft that combines the speed of an aircraft with the landing versatility of an helicopter. First conceived in 1983, built by the Bell and Boeing corporations the prototype flew in 1989.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bell-Boeing_V-22_Osprey The Osprey began to phase out the older Sea Knight helicopters in the U.S. Marine inventory in 2007 after some setbacks during testing. The V-22 Makes some discreet appearances in [[Evangelion 1.0]]. The Osprey also appears in [[Evangelion 2.0]] by delivering personnel to the Eva-03's activation test, as well as appearances in the [[Evangelion 3.0]] teaser at the end of 2.0. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 2 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:Eva2-22 C1159.jpg|250px|]] |image2 = [[Image:V22 07.jpg|250px]] |caption = V-22 Osprey in [[Evangelion 2.0]] and in real life. }} {{-}} === Yakovlev Yak-38 === The soviet built Yak-38 appears in [[Episode 8]]. The Yak-38 is a VTOL fighter plane developed in 1971 with a total of 231 built.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yakovlev_Yak-38 The variant of the aircraft used appears to be the Yak-38U which is the only version of the aircraft which has a crew of two.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yakovlev_Yak-38#Variants However, Kensuke refers to the variant in-Episode as a "Yak-38 ''kai'' -- denoting "modified" or "special", with the English dub translating this as "custom" -- suggesting that the Yak-38 Kaji flies in is a heavily modified or custom-made variant of the Yak-38U. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 3 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:08 C215c.jpg|250px|]] |image2 = [[Image:08 C220b.jpg|250px]] |image3 = [[Image:Yak38 10.jpg|250px]] |caption = Yak-38 in [[Episode 8]] and in real life. }} {{-}} === Sikorsky CH-53E Super Stallion === The CH-53E is a large multipurpose helicopter based off of the older CH-53. It has a large internal and external cargo capacity allowing it to transport 32,000 lbs of external cargo or 30,000 lbs of internal cargo, which in terms of personnel would be 55 soldiers. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CH-53E_Super_Stallion The CH-53E makes appearances in [[Episode 12]] which appears to be operated by the [[JSSDF]] and in [[Episode 16]] where it is a Nerv operated CH-53E. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 3 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:12 C133b.jpg|250px|]] |image2 = [[Image:16 C138b.jpg|250px]] |image3 = [[Image:CH53E.jpg|250px]] |caption = CH-53E in episodes 12, 16 and in real life. }} {{-}} === Beriev A-40 Albatross === The Beriev A-40 Albatross, NATO reporting name "Mermaid", is a jet powered flying boat built for the Soviet Navy for anti-submarine warfare. It first flew in 1986, and shortly after the first flight the project was canceled with one plane built and another almost completed. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beriev_A-40 The Nerv operated A-40 appears in [[Evangelion 2.0]] transporting Kaji from [[Bethany Base]]. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 2 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:Eva2-22 C0069.jpg|250px|]] |image2 = [[Image:A-40 Beriev.jpg|250px]] |image3 = |caption = A-40 in Evangelion 2.0 and in real life. }} {{-}} === Lun class ekranoplan (MD-160) === The Lun class ekranoplan , NATO reporting name "Duck", is a jet powered seaplane built for the Soviet Navy. It is equipped with six guided missiles mounted in tubes on top of the fuselage and entered service in 1987 with a total of one built. The sole unit was removed from service sometime in the 1990s and has been laid up in a harbor since. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lun-class_ekranoplan The Lun appears in [[Evangelion 1.0]] transporting Misato, Ritsuko, and Maya from the site of Sachiel's suicide following the recovery of Unit-01. The version used by Nerv appears to be solely a transport vehicle since it has the six missiles removed from the fuselage. {{MontageBox |float = left |columns = 3 |title = |colour = |image1 = [[Image:Lun Ekranoplan.jpg|250px]] |image2 = [[Image:Eva1.01-C439.jpg|250px]] |image3 = [[Image:Lunclasspresentday.jpg|250px]] |caption = Lun in it's prime, in Evangelion 1.0, present day. }} {{-}}

=== Lockheed P-3 Orion === The P-3 Orion built by Lockheed Martin is a turboprop maritime aircraft that is used for maritime patrols, intelligence gathering, anti-submarine, and anti-ship warfare. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/P-3_Orion The Orion appears in Evangelion 1.0 providing surveillance on [[Ramiel]]. ==Boats and Small Watercraft== == References == {{reflist}} [[Category:A to Z]] [[Category:Real World References]] [[Category:Vehicles]]
html:
A myriad of real world vehicles can be seen throughout Neon Genesis Evangelion, End of Evangelion, and Rebuild of Evangelion. These are usually military vehicles, sometimes civilian, but always drawn and animated with great detail to their features.

Evangelion has a special relationship with warships, which go on their own page, although boats and small watercraft will remain on this page. See Warships in Evangelion for details on the warships of the series.

Contents

  • 1 Ground vehicles
    • 1.1 Type-74 Nana-yon
    • 1.2 Type 90 MBT
    • 1.3 T-80
    • 1.4 BM-30 Smerch
    • 1.5 M-270 MLRS (Multiple Launch Rocket System)
    • 1.6 AMX-10 RC
    • 1.7 Komatsu LAV
  • 2 Aircraft
    • 2.1 Sukhoi Su-33 Flanker-D
    • 2.2 Sukhoi Su-34 Fullback
    • 2.3 Bell-Boeing V-22 Osprey
    • 2.4 Yakovlev Yak-38
    • 2.5 Sikorsky CH-53E Super Stallion
    • 2.6 Beriev A-40 Albatross
    • 2.7 Lun class ekranoplan (MD-160)
    • 2.8 Lockheed P-3 Orion
  • 3 Boats and Small Watercraft
  • 4 References

Ground vehicles[edit]

Type-74 Nana-yon[edit]

The tank most commonly seen in the series, the Type-74[1] is the main battle tank of the JGSDF with 700 deployed as of 2006. It was originally designed during the 1960s, an era of tank design where it was assumed that HEAT (High-Explosive Anti-Tank) rounds had made most armor obsolete, and as such it has relatively thin armor that made the tank almost completely obsolete before the first production models even rolled off the assembly line in 1975. It is primarily armed with a Royal Ordnance L7 105 mm rifled gun, sports 120mm of armor, carries a crew of four, can travel at a speed of 53 km/h, and weighs 38 tonnes. In Explanation of Evangelion 1.01 it's called Type-76 Kai (modifed).

It can be seen in Episode 1, Episode 9, End of Evangelion, Evangelion 1.0, and Evangelion 2.0.

Type-74 tanks from Episode #01.




Type 90 MBT[edit]

This tank has been misidentified as a multitude of vehicles, among them a Leopard 2 A10 (fictional variant of the German Leopard 2, a British Challenger 2, both based on the turret shape. However, turret armor is often and drastically changed across different versions (one such example being the Leopard 2 A4 compared to the A5), so the deciding factor is the layout of the hull, most notable features being the width to height ratio of the hull, the position of the driver's hatch, headlights and tow hooks.

In real life, the Type 90's design is quite similar to that of the Leopard 2, having very similar armor layout to the A4 and using the same 120mm Rheinmetall L44 smoothbore gun also used by the American M1 Abrams, South Korean K1A1 and older versions of the Leopard 2. What sets it apart from other MBTs is its suspension system - a hydraulic one allowing for the hull to tilt forwards and backwards in order to reduce its profile when cresting hills - and the main gun having a conveyor belt style autoloader, reducing the crew to three men compared to the four most other MBTs have. The exact composition of the armor is a tightly kept secret, but it follows the principle of modular composite armor: Blocks of layered ceramic and steel plates intended to shatter impact rounds and dissipate the effect of hollow charges, that can be individually replaced when damaged, even as a field repair. Due to the overpressure system inside the crew compartment, the tank is NBC (nuclear, biological, chemical) proof, meaning it can withstand and protect the crew from gas attacks and even the aftereffects of a nuclear strike.

It can be seen in Evangelion 1.0 and Evangelion 2.0.



T-80[edit]

The T-80[2] is a Soviet main battle tank developed from the T-64[3], it entered service in 1976 and it was the first production tank in the world using a gas turbine engine for its propulsion, a full four years before the first appearance of the US M1 Abrams. The Russian army discontinued procurement and development of this tank because of its poor performance during the First Chechen War, but it's still under production for export, and diesel power variants with a much lower fuel consumption (one of the type's principal problems, shared with all gas turbine tanks) have been developed. The version shown in Evangelion 2.0 is called T-80UN in the Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection, it's a fictional version.

It can be seen in Evangelion 2.0 during the battle against the Third Angel.



BM-30 Smerch[edit]

The BM-30 "Smerch"[4] (tornado) is a Russian-built multiple rocket launcher first designed in the 1980s that is currently used by 13 different countries, and whose Russian designation is 9A52. It is capable of firing 12 300mm missiles and rockets in 38 seconds over a maximum distance of 90km, with a total reload time of twenty minutes. It requires a crew of three, weighs 43.7 tons, and has a maximum speed of 60 km/h.

It can be briefly seen in Evangelion 1.0 during the initial attack on Sachiel.

Smerch in Evangelion 1.0 and in real life




M-270 MLRS (Multiple Launch Rocket System)[edit]

The iconic M270[5], often referred to simply as the MLRS, is an American-made rocket artillery system first delivered to the US Army in 1983 that has since come to be used by 12 different countries, including 99 in use by the JGSDF. It can fire 12 rockets in less then 40 seconds or 2 missiles every ten seconds, and can be reloaded in either 4 minutes (M270) or 3 minutes (M270A1). It requires a crew of three, weighs 27.5 tons, and has a maximum speed of 50 mph (80.5 km/h).



AMX-10 RC[edit]

A French tank destroyer and recon vehicle. [6]

Several of these can be seen in EoE being used by the JSSDF to assault Nerv's defenses.



Komatsu LAV[edit]

A Japanese light armored vehicle built by Komatsu Ltd. Defense Systems Division for the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force. [7] It appears in Evangelion 1.0.

Aircraft[edit]

Sukhoi Su-33 Flanker-D[edit]

A Russian Navy fighter first introduced in 1994, the Su-33[8] can be seen prominently in Episode 08. This is rather different from the Su-33's real-life production run being limited to 36 aircraft (excluding prototypes). The animators have very faithfully depicted the nose landing gear of the Su-33 as seen on the right of the image from Episode 08, but this fidelity to detail ironically makes it clear that the STOBAR aircraft can't operate from an American Nimitz-class carrier in that configuration; they lack an American-pattern "tow bar" catapult attachment to their nose landing gear.

Su-33s from Episode #08 compared with real-life Su-33s. Note the highly detailed landing gear on the Episode 08 image to the left, and the absence of a "tow bar" catapult attachment as seen on US carrier-borne aircraft.


Sukhoi Su-34 Fullback[edit]

A twin-seat fighter bomber of the Russian Air Force. It first flew in 1990 and entered production in 2004 incorporating design aspects from other Sukhoi fighters including the Su-33.[9] It appears in Evangelion 2.0 in the role of escorting Unit-02 to Japan. And despite the fact that it is not designed for carrier flight operations it appears at the end of Evangelin 2.0 in the 3.0 next time preview on a Nimitz-class aircraft carrier. (It is worth noting that the animators misjudged the size of the aircraft - the aircraft seen on the carrier are approximately 70 percent the size of their real-world counterparts).

Su-34s from Evangelion 2.0 compared with real-life Su-34.


Bell-Boeing V-22 Osprey[edit]

An American multi-purpose tilt rotor aircraft that combines the speed of an aircraft with the landing versatility of an helicopter. First conceived in 1983, built by the Bell and Boeing corporations the prototype flew in 1989.[10] The Osprey began to phase out the older Sea Knight helicopters in the U.S. Marine inventory in 2007 after some setbacks during testing. The V-22 Makes some discreet appearances in Evangelion 1.0. The Osprey also appears in Evangelion 2.0 by delivering personnel to the Eva-03's activation test, as well as appearances in the Evangelion 3.0 teaser at the end of 2.0.

V-22 Osprey in Evangelion 2.0 and in real life.



Yakovlev Yak-38[edit]

The soviet built Yak-38 appears in Episode 8. The Yak-38 is a VTOL fighter plane developed in 1971 with a total of 231 built.[11] The variant of the aircraft used appears to be the Yak-38U which is the only version of the aircraft which has a crew of two.[12] However, Kensuke refers to the variant in-Episode as a "Yak-38 kai -- denoting "modified" or "special", with the English dub translating this as "custom" -- suggesting that the Yak-38 Kaji flies in is a heavily modified or custom-made variant of the Yak-38U.

Yak-38 in Episode 8 and in real life.



Sikorsky CH-53E Super Stallion[edit]

The CH-53E is a large multipurpose helicopter based off of the older CH-53. It has a large internal and external cargo capacity allowing it to transport 32,000 lbs of external cargo or 30,000 lbs of internal cargo, which in terms of personnel would be 55 soldiers. [13] The CH-53E makes appearances in Episode 12 which appears to be operated by the JSSDF and in Episode 16 where it is a Nerv operated CH-53E.

CH-53E in episodes 12, 16 and in real life.



Beriev A-40 Albatross[edit]

The Beriev A-40 Albatross, NATO reporting name "Mermaid", is a jet powered flying boat built for the Soviet Navy for anti-submarine warfare. It first flew in 1986, and shortly after the first flight the project was canceled with one plane built and another almost completed. [14] The Nerv operated A-40 appears in Evangelion 2.0 transporting Kaji from Bethany Base.

A-40 in Evangelion 2.0 and in real life.



Lun class ekranoplan (MD-160)[edit]

The Lun class ekranoplan , NATO reporting name "Duck", is a jet powered seaplane built for the Soviet Navy. It is equipped with six guided missiles mounted in tubes on top of the fuselage and entered service in 1987 with a total of one built. The sole unit was removed from service sometime in the 1990s and has been laid up in a harbor since. [15] The Lun appears in Evangelion 1.0 transporting Misato, Ritsuko, and Maya from the site of Sachiel's suicide following the recovery of Unit-01. The version used by Nerv appears to be solely a transport vehicle since it has the six missiles removed from the fuselage.


Lun in it's prime, in Evangelion 1.0, present day.




Lockheed P-3 Orion[edit]

The P-3 Orion built by Lockheed Martin is a turboprop maritime aircraft that is used for maritime patrols, intelligence gathering, anti-submarine, and anti-ship warfare. [16] The Orion appears in Evangelion 1.0 providing surveillance on Ramiel.

Boats and Small Watercraft[edit]

  9. ↑ http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sukhoi_Su-34

  11. ↑ http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yakovlev_Yak-38

chunks.content:
A myriad of real world vehicles can be seen throughout Neon Genesis Evangelion, End of Evangelion, and Rebuild of Evangelion. These are usually military vehicles, sometimes civilian, but always drawn and animated with great detail to their features.

Evangelion has a special relationship with warships, which go on their own page, although boats and small watercraft will remain on this page. See Warships in Evangelion for details on the warships of the series.

In Explanation of Evangelion 1.01 it's called Type-76 Kai (modifed).

's design is quite similar to that of the Leopard 2, having very similar armor layout to the A4 and using the same 120mm Rheinmetall L44 smoothbore gun also used by the American M1 Abrams, South Korean K1A1 and older versions of the Leopard 2.
What sets it apart from other MBTs is its suspension system - a hydraulic one allowing for the hull to tilt forwards and backwards in order to reduce its profile when cresting hills - and the main gun having a conveyor belt style autoloader, reducing the crew to three men compared to the four most other MBTs have.

Due to the overpressure system inside the crew compartment, the tank is NBC (nuclear, biological, chemical) proof, meaning it can withstand and protect the crew from gas attacks and even the aftereffects of a nuclear strike.

none|thumb|250px|Several Type 90s open fire in Evangelion 1.0. Note the reactive armor plates bolted onto the chassis and turret

 T-80 

The T-80http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T-80 is a Soviet main battle tank developed from the T-64http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T-64, it entered service in 1976 and it was the first production tank in the world using a gas turbine engine for its propulsion, a full four years before the first appearance of the US M1 Abrams. The Russian army discontinued procurement and development of this tank because of its poor performance during the First Chechen War, but it's still under production for export, and diesel power variants with a much lower fuel consumption (one of the type's principal problems, shared with all gas turbine tanks) have been developed. The version shown in Evangelion 2.0 is called T-80UN in the Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection, it's a fictional version.

It can be seen in Evangelion 2.0 during the battle against the Third Angel.

 BM-30 Smerch 

The BM-30 "Smerch"http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BM-30_Smerch (tornado) is a Russian-built multiple rocket launcher first designed in the 1980s that is currently used by 13 different countries, and whose Russian designation is 9A52. It is capable of firing 12 300mm missiles and rockets in 38 seconds over a maximum distance of 90km, with a total reload time of twenty minutes. It requires a crew of three, weighs 43.7 tons, and has a maximum speed of 60 km/h.

It can be briefly seen in Evangelion 1.0 during the initial attack on Sachiel. 

 M-270 MLRS (Multiple Launch Rocket System) 

The iconic M270http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M270_Multiple_Launch_Rocket_System, often referred to simply as the MLRS, is an American-made rocket artillery system first delivered to the US Army in 1983 that has since come to be used by 12 different countries, including 99 in use by the JGSDF. It can fire 12 rockets in less then 40 seconds or 2 missiles every ten seconds, and can be reloaded in either 4 minutes (M270) or 3 minutes (M270A1). It requires a crew of three, weighs 27.5 tons, and has a maximum speed of 50 mph (80.5 km/h).

 Komatsu LAV 
A Japanese light armored vehicle built by Komatsu Ltd. Defense Systems Division for the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Komatsu_LAV It appears in Evangelion 1.0. 

 Aircraft  

 Sukhoi Su-33 Flanker-D 

A Russian Navy fighter first introduced in 1994, the Su-33http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sukhoi_Su-33 can be seen prominently in Episode 08. This is rather different from the Su-33's real-life production run being limited to 36 aircraft (excluding prototypes). The animators have very faithfully depicted the nose landing gear of the Su-33 as seen on the right of the image from Episode 08, but this fidelity to detail ironically makes it clear that the STOBAR aircraft can't operate from an American Nimitz-class carrier in that configuration; they lack an American-pattern "tow bar" catapult attachment to their nose landing gear.  
	

 Sukhoi Su-34 Fullback 

A twin-seat fighter bomber of the Russian Air Force. It first flew in 1990 and entered production in 2004 incorporating design aspects from other Sukhoi fighters including the Su-33.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sukhoi_Su-34 It appears in Evangelion 2.0 in the role of escorting Unit-02 to Japan. And despite the fact that it is not designed for carrier flight operations it appears at the end of Evangelin 2.0 in the 3.0 next time preview on a Nimitz-class aircraft carrier. (It is worth noting that the animators misjudged the size of the aircraft - the aircraft seen on the carrier are approximately 70 percent the size of their real-world counterparts).

 Bell-Boeing V-22 Osprey 

An American multi-purpose tilt rotor aircraft that combines the speed of an aircraft with the landing versatility of an helicopter. First conceived in 1983, built by the Bell and Boeing corporations the prototype flew in 1989.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bell-Boeing_V-22_Osprey The Osprey began to phase out the older Sea Knight helicopters in the U.S. Marine inventory in 2007 after some setbacks during testing. The V-22 Makes some discreet appearances in Evangelion 1.0. The Osprey also appears in

y began to phase out the older Sea Knight helicopters in the U.S. Marine inventory in 2007 after some setbacks during testing. The V-22 Makes some discreet appearances in Evangelion 1.0. The Osprey also appears in Evangelion 2.0 by delivering personnel to the Eva-03's activation test, as well as appearances in the Evangelion 3.0 teaser at the end of 2.0.

 Yakovlev Yak-38 

The soviet built Yak-38 appears in Episode 8. The Yak-38 is a VTOL fighter plane developed in 1971 with a total of 231 built.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yakovlev_Yak-38 The variant of the aircraft used appears to be the Yak-38U which is the only version of the aircraft which has a crew of two.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yakovlev_Yak-38#Variants However, Kensuke refers to the variant in-Episode as a "Yak-38 kai -- denoting "modified" or "special", with the English dub translating this as "custom" -- suggesting that the Yak-38 Kaji flies in is a heavily modified or custom-made variant of the Yak-38U. 

 Sikorsky CH-53E Super Stallion 

The CH-53E is a large multipurpose helicopter based off of the older CH-53. It has a large internal and external cargo capacity allowing it to transport 32,000 lbs of external cargo or 30,000 lbs of internal cargo, which in terms of personnel would be 55 soldiers. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CH-53E_Super_Stallion The CH-53E makes appearances in Episode 12 which appears to be operated by the JSSDF and in Episode 16 where it is a Nerv operated CH-53E.

 Beriev A-40 Albatross 
The Beriev A-40 Albatross, NATO reporting name "Mermaid", is a jet powered flying boat built for the Soviet Navy for anti-submarine warfare. It first flew in 1986, and shortly after the first flight the project was canceled with one plane built and another almost completed. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beriev_A-40 The Nerv operated A-40 appears in Evangelion 2.0 transporting Kaji from Bethany Base. 

 Lun class ekranoplan (MD-160) 
The Lun class ekranoplan , NATO reporting name

eriev_A-40 The Nerv operated A-40 appears in Evangelion 2.0 transporting Kaji from Bethany Base. 

 Lun class ekranoplan (MD-160) 
The Lun class ekranoplan , NATO reporting name "Duck", is a jet powered seaplane built for the Soviet Navy. It is equipped with six guided missiles mounted in tubes on top of the fuselage and entered service in 1987 with a total of one built. The sole unit was removed from service sometime in the 1990s and has been laid up in a harbor since. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lun-class_ekranoplan The Lun appears in Evangelion 1.0 transporting Misato, Ritsuko, and Maya from the site of Sachiel's suicide following the recovery of Unit-01. The version used by Nerv appears to be solely a transport vehicle since it has the six missiles removed from the fuselage.

 Lockheed P-3 Orion 
The P-3 Orion built by Lockheed Martin is a turboprop maritime aircraft that is used for maritime patrols, intelligence gathering, anti-submarine, and anti-ship warfare. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/P-3_Orion The Orion appears in Evangelion 1.0 providing surveillance on Ramiel.
